assertions.title: End-to-end keystroke renders without frame drops

steps.observed: Build succeeded (exit 0, already up to date)

steps.action: Launch QEMU with gic-version=3, 9p, GPU, keyboard
steps.observed: QEMU started (PID 78754), alive after 8s boot wait

steps.action: Wait 2s, capture framebuffer screenshot via screendump
steps.observed: PPM captured and converted to PNG
Text 'helloworldtriplebuf' visible with cursor at end, clock showing 08:26:21 in top-right

steps.action: Check serial log for error patterns (copy_front_to_back, failed, panic, crash)
steps.observed: No error patterns found in serial log

assertions.title: Stress test passes — 60+ seconds sustained rapid input on 4 SMP cores

steps.action: Run stress.sh (180s timeout, headless QEMU, 4 SMP cores)
steps.expected: Fuzz test and stress test pass with exit code 0
steps.observed: ALL PASS after 68s — fuzz test PASS, stress test PASS, exit code 0

steps.action: Check for crash markers (panicking, BUG:, FAIL)
steps.observed: No crash markers detected; script monitored serial output throughout and found none

summary: Tested 2 assertions: 2 passed. VAL-CROSS-001 confirmed all 20 keystrokes rendered on screen with no frame drops and no error patterns in serial. VAL-CROSS-006 confirmed stress test passed after 68s on 4 SMP cores with no crashes, panics, or deadlocks under the triple-buffer protocol.
